seo.description: Lakshay Kumar is a Generative AI engineer and founder in Bangalore. Full-time at Zato. Shipping DocIQ and Hyd/Rent on the side. Always up for a good conversation about AI, products, and the messy middle.

cta.secondary: Working full-time at Zato — always up for a good conversation. No pitch, no retainer.

intro.tagline: Generative AI engineer and founder. Building AI that feels human.

highlight.title: DocIQ
highlight.description: Turn static PDFs into interactive, chat-ready documents. Built for teams who read more than they write.

highlight.title: Hyd/Rent
highlight.description: A unified rental platform for Hyderabad — one place for homes, roommates, deposits, and listings that don't waste your evening.

summary: I build AI products end-to-end — from the prompt layer to the API surface to the interface the user actually sees. Five years in, shipped at six companies, launched a dozen products across AI, SaaS, and automation.
I care about the small moments: a loading state that tells the truth, a prompt that returns in one try, a page that loads before you notice. The craft is in the friction you remove.
My work sits at the intersection of LLMs, product thinking, and fast iteration. I write Python and TypeScript, prototype in a day, ship in a week, and iterate with the user in the room.
Currently Generative AI Engineer at Zato and founder of DocIQ. Previously shipped at GAIT Global, Atria Foundation, and HealthArk Insights.
about.identity: Generative AI engineer and founder who turns vague ideas into working products — fast, deliberate, human.

skill_set: Agentic workflows that actually finish the task
Prompts tuned for cost, latency, and accuracy
Turning weekend ideas into shipped products

offerings.title: Generative AI & LLM Systems
offerings.description: Design and deploy production-grade LLM systems — RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and conversational interfaces with Claude, GPT-4, and open-source models. Tuned for cost, latency, and reliability.

offerings.title: AI Product Thinking
offerings.description: I bring builder instincts to early-stage thinking — scope, model choice, UX, and launch plan. The kind of feedback that helps your first version actually ship.

offerings.title: Full-Stack Engineering
offerings.description: FastAPI, Python, TypeScript, React, Next.js. Clean backends, considered frontends, observable infrastructure. The kind of codebase future-you can extend.

offerings.title: Automation & Agentic Workflows
offerings.description: Turning manual toil into intelligent automations. LangChain, function-calling, tool use, and structured output. Humans stay in the loop where it matters.

offerings.title: Product Building, 0 → 1
offerings.description: Founder-mode execution on my own things — DocIQ, Hyd/Rent. Architecture, AI feature prioritization, launch copy that sounds human, and the loop between ship-and-learn.

automation: Agent design (tool use, function calling, ReAct)

experiments.section_title: Things I tried, and what they taught me.

projects.name: Techuro
projects.lesson: Strong vision isn't enough. Without early product-market fit and clear distribution, momentum dies in the demo.

projects.name: Eve-Link
projects.lesson: Changing live networking behavior is brutally hard. Tech can assist the moment, not replace it.

projects.name: NFC Smart Cards
projects.lesson: Cool hardware means nothing without a must-have use case and easy to copy.

projects.name: Linky
projects.description: Job application automation for early-career engineers. Learned that speed without signal hurts more than it helps.

faqs.a: Generative AI products and features — LLM integrations, agentic workflows, RAG pipelines, and the full-stack surface around them. At Zato I work on AI infrastructure and products. On the side I ship my own things — DocIQ (an interactive PDF assistant) and Hyd/Rent (a unified rental platform for Hyderabad).

Yes — always up for a good conversation. I'm full-time at Zato, but I love meeting other builders, engineers, founders, and anyone doing interesting work in AI or products. No pitch required. Just say hi.

faqs.q: What kinds of conversations do you enjoy?
faqs.a: Early product ideas you're stuck on, LLM architecture choices, prompt evaluation strategies, the pain of shipping AI features, and the messy middle of going 0→1. I'll share what I know honestly — and tell you when I don't.

faqs.q: Where are you based and how do you work?
faqs.a: Bangalore, India (UTC+5:30). I collaborate with folks globally — mostly async, Loom-friendly, comfortable overlapping a few hours. Happy to jump on a call or just trade voice notes.

faqs.q: Which models and stacks do you work with?
faqs.a: Claude (Anthropic), GPT-4 and GPT-4o (OpenAI), plus open-source Llama and Mistral models. Orchestration with LangChain and LlamaIndex. On the app side: Python, FastAPI, TypeScript, React, Next.js, PostgreSQL, and vector databases like Pinecone and Weaviate. I pick the stack that ships fastest for the constraints at hand.

Informally, yes — I enjoy talking shop with other engineers, early-career devs, and first-time founders. If you're building something in AI or full-stack product land and want a second opinion, drop a message.

faqs.q: What's the fastest way to reach you?
faqs.a: The form below, or email directly — hey@lakshaykumar.tech. I reply to everything within 24 hours. LinkedIn DMs also work if you prefer.
